100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 22, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, discussing the escalating tensions in Europe as various nations jockey for power in the wake of World War I. In the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition intensified, with rising calls for repeal amid increasing public discontent. Meanwhile, in Egypt, tensions mounted as nationalist sentiments grew against British rule, leading to widespread protests and calls for independence.
